objective: Stand up the telemetry substrate (OTLP + Jaeger) AND one bare iroh-backed gateway node that emits a complete boot-span chain. Telemetry IS the deliverable; the gateway is the demo that proves the telemetry pipeline works.
in_scope: crates/rafka-telemetry/ — init_telemetry(service_name) returns a guard; uses tracing-opentelemetry + opentelemetry-otlp; BatchSpanProcessor; TelemetryGuard flushes on drop
crates/rafka-mesh-transport/ — minimal stub: MeshTransport trait + IrohMeshTransport that just wraps iroh::Endpoint create + a no-op accept loop. Every async fn is #[instrument].
gateway/ — one binary. main() calls init_telemetry first. Boot sequence emits 6 spans under a single rafka.mesh.node.ready parent. Idles emitting rafka.mesh.heartbeat every 5s.
Signal handler — SIGINT/SIGTERM emits rafka.mesh.node.stopping span before graceful exit
out_of_scope: deployment/dev/docker-compose.otlp.yml — DELETED from scope; reusing shared rafka-test-jaeger OTLP/gRPC on localhost:4316 (OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT) and Jaeger UI on localhost:16686
Tests (formal test coverage skipped for pilot phase per Principle #6 — telemetry IS the verification)
ANY HTTP route on the gateway binary
ANY app logic (no topics, no records, no authz, nothing)
Custom QUIC code or hand-rolled gossip
exit_criteria: cargo run -p rafka-gateway boots without panic and stays alive (idles emitting heartbeats)
Second invocation of the binary reads the SAME identity (no re-mint)

Jaeger UI search for service=rafkav2-data-gateway shows a trace with: rafka.mesh.node.ready (root) → rafka.mesh.boot.identity_loaded (or .minted) → rafka.mesh.boot.endpoint_created → rafka.mesh.boot.alpn_registered → rafka.mesh.boot.gossip_started → rafka.mesh.boot.accept_loop_started
While idle, rafka.mesh.heartbeat span appears every ~5s in Jaeger
Killing the gateway process (Ctrl+C or kill) emits a final rafka.mesh.node.stopping span before exit
Grep test: zero hand-rolled mesh primitives (no fn.*gossip / fn.*discover_peer / fn.*nat_traverse outside iroh dep)
Engineer's final SendMessage includes a clickable Jaeger URL pre-filtered to service=rafkav2-data-gateway
discipline: Telemetry is built FIRST. The OTLP collector + telemetry crate land before any iroh code. No code without spans.
Every async fn in every new crate is #[instrument]-decorated.
Every state transition is a span. Every error is a span attribute.
No defer language. Full scope in one merge.
No Claude attribution in commit messages.
Workspace gate zero/zero before every commit.
Sprint closes with engineer surfacing a clickable Jaeger URL — without URL, sprint is not closed.

infrastructure.note: Reuses shared rafka-test-jaeger on port 4316 (OTLP/gRPC). Service name prefixed rafkav2- to avoid collisions with v1 spans. All config via env vars per Principle #8.
